description: ArcheryEdge knowledge base schema — recurve archery coaching chunks extracted from YouTube videos

chunk.chunk_id: string — unique identifier, format: {source_id}_{topic_code}_{index}, e.g. vid001_stance_003

source.video_id: string — YouTube video ID, e.g. dQw4w9WgXcQ
source.video_title: string — title of the YouTube video
source.timestamp_start: number — start time in seconds where this tip appears in the video

content.topic: string — one of: stance, draw_anchor, grip, aiming, release_followthrough, breathing_focus, equipment, common_mistakes
content.subtopic: string — more specific label, e.g. 'shoulder alignment', 'back tension', 'finger placement'
content.skill_level: string — one of: beginner, intermediate, advanced, all
content.style: string — one of: recurve, compound, traditional, general
content.tip: string — the coaching tip itself, 1-3 sentences, written as direct actionable advice
content.mistake_addressed: string or null — the specific error this tip corrects, if applicable
keywords: array of strings — key terms for retrieval, e.g. elbow, anchor, draw weight

joints_involved: array — relevant body joints, e.g. shoulder, elbow, wrist, hip, knee
pose_relevance.measurable: boolean — true if this tip can be verified by pose estimation metrics
pose_relevance.metric_hint: string or null — what to measure, e.g. 'draw elbow angle should be 160-180 degrees', null if not measurable

metadata.gemini_model: string — Gemini model used for extraction

source.video_title: Recurve Archery for Beginners — Perfect Stance

content.tip: Stand perpendicular to the target with your feet shoulder-width apart, straddling the shooting line. Your weight should be evenly distributed across both feet — avoid leaning forward or backward.
content.mistake_addressed: Uneven weight distribution causing inconsistent arrow grouping

pose_relevance.metric_hint: Hip-to-ankle vertical alignment should be within 5 degrees of vertical
